findings.message: Stale derived field: rename_item() updates item.name without updating slug, violating the documented invariant that slug must stay in sync with name. The route bypasses repository.update_item_name() which correctly maintains both fields together.
findings.suggestion: Replace the direct ORM manipulation with a call to the repository helper: from ..db.repository import update_item_name; then call update_item_name(session, item_id, payload.name) instead of manually setting item.name and committing. This ensures slug remains synchronized.

findings.message: Docstring documents a data consistency bug: 'Updates only item.name; slug is not recalculated so it diverges from name.' This explicit documentation of broken behavior indicates the derived field invariant is being knowingly violated.
findings.suggestion: Remove this docstring or update it to accurately describe correct behavior. Either way, fix the underlying implementation to use update_item_name() from the repository layer.
